name: metr-la

env_axis.name: city
env_axis.shift_mechanism: single city here; cross-city realized when paired with PEMS-BAY (Bay Area) or extended to Chengdu/Shenzhen

ood_protocol.name: metr-cross-city
ood_protocol.ref: Li et al. 2018 ICLR (DCRNN); Liu et al. 2023 CIKM (FEPCross)

hypotheses: METR-LA → PEMS-BAY is a weak shift (both California freeway, similar urbanization), exposing the threshold below which 'cross-city' is indistinguishable from 'cross-fold'.
Adding Chengdu/Shenzhen as test envs separates 'cross-administrative-unit' from 'cross-traffic-culture' shift.
intuitions: Cross-city baselines from Liu 2023 CIKM and ECML/PKDD 2024 FEPCross likely exist.
2024-25 traffic-foundation-model work aggregating all four cities probably exists.
unknowns: Network topology (which sensor connects to which) is supplied externally as adjacency, not discovered from the data — there is no established label for 'imposed-vs-discovered topology' as a shift-relevant axis.
Within-day periodicity dominates variance; distinguishing temporal-shift signal from periodic baseline is itself unsolved here.
epistemic_state.boundary: Ty=1, C=0; restricted to spatial-temporal OOD. Single measurement modality (speed only — no flow, no occupancy).

representational_commitments: Adjacency matrix supplied externally (not derived from co-movement)
